title: User Onboarding & Education
description: Add interactive onboarding experiences and educational features that help new users learn Jarvy quickly and effectively, reducing time-to-productivity and support burden
acceptance_criteria: jarvy init detects existing project files and suggests appropriate stacks
jarvy init provides selection UI for tools and generates valid jarvy.toml
jarvy init supports --template for non-interactive use
jarvy quickstart runs system compatibility check
jarvy quickstart guides through config creation or import
jarvy quickstart completes in under 3 minutes
jarvy templates lists all available templates with descriptions
jarvy templates show displays detailed tool list for each template
jarvy templates use creates valid jarvy.toml from template
First-run detection shows welcome banner once for new users
First-run detection does not show in CI environments
All interactive prompts work in all terminals with fallback for non-TTY

tasks.title: Create onboarding module structure
tasks.description: Create src/onboarding/ module with first-run detection and welcome banner
subtasks: Implement is_first_run() detection using marker file
Implement mark_initialized() to set marker after first run
Create welcome banner with clear action suggestions
Add dismissible permanently via config setting

tasks.title: Implement project type detection
tasks.description: Detect existing project files to suggest appropriate development stack
subtasks: Return suggested stack based on detection

tasks.title: Implement jarvy init wizard
tasks.description: Interactive wizard to create jarvy.toml for the current project
subtasks: Show clear next steps after creation

tasks.title: Create template schema and loader
tasks.description: Define template TOML schema and implement template loading
subtasks: Define Template struct with name, description, category, tags
Define tool list with versions and purposes
Define meta section with author, version, min_jarvy_version

tasks.title: Create built-in templates
tasks.description: Add 20 curated templates for common development stacks

tasks.title: Implement jarvy templates command
tasks.description: Browse and use pre-built configuration templates
subtasks: Implement templates list with categories and descriptions
Implement templates show <name> with detailed tool list
Implement templates use <name> to create jarvy.toml
Support --output path for custom output location
Support --setup to create and install immediately

tasks.title: Implement jarvy quickstart flow
tasks.description: Guided first-run experience for new Jarvy users
subtasks: Implement system compatibility check (OS, package manager, shell)
Offer options: create new config, import from existing, use template, skip
Offer immediate setup option after config creation
Show useful commands and documentation links
Ensure quickstart completes in under 3 minutes

tasks.title: Integrate first-run detection
tasks.description: Add first-run welcome banner to CLI commands
subtasks: Show welcome banner with quick action suggestions
Do not show in CI environments (CI=true)
Allow dismissing permanently via jarvy config set show_welcome false

tasks.title: Write unit tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for onboarding modules
subtasks: Test project type detection for each supported language
Test template schema parsing and validation

tasks.title: Write integration tests
tasks.description: Create integration tests for onboarding commands
subtasks: Test jarvy templates use creates valid jarvy.toml
Test jarvy quickstart in non-interactive mode

tasks.title: Update documentation
subtasks: Document jarvy init command and options

implementation_notes.interactive_prompts: Use inquire crate for Select and MultiSelect prompts
implementation_notes.non_tty_fallback: Require --non-interactive flag when stdin is not TTY
implementation_notes.template_storage: Templates stored as static data in src/templates/builtin.rs

config_example.template_format:
[template]
name = "react"
description = "Complete React development environment"
category = "Web Development"

[tools]
node = "20"
git = "latest"
config_example.detection_markers: package.json -> Node.js, Cargo.toml -> Rust, go.mod -> Go

risks.mitigation: Fallback to simple prompts, require --non-interactive flag for non-TTY
Version templates, automated testing, use 'latest' for most tools
Always allow user override, show what was detected
One-time welcome, --quiet flag, direct command access
